abstract: Called when content starts arriving for a page load.

declaration.code: optional func webView(_ sender: WebView!, didCommitLoadFor frame: WebFrame!)

overview:
## Discussion

This method is invoked when a data source transitions from a provisional to committed state—that is, once the data source of `frame` has received one byte or more of data. This method is invoked after a [doc://com.apple.webkit/documentation/WebKit/WebFrameLoadDelegate/webView(_:didStartProvisionalLoadFor:)] message but before a `webView:didFinishLoadForFrame:` message is sent to the delegate.

In some cases, a single frame load may be committed more than once. This happens in the case of multipart/x-mixed-replace, also known as a “server push.” In this case, a single frame load results in multiple documents loaded in sequence. This method is invoked once for each document that is successfully loaded.

# webView(_:didCommitLoadFor:)

**Instance Method**

Called when content starts arriving for a page load.

## Declaration

```swift
optional func webView(_ sender: WebView!, didCommitLoadFor frame: WebFrame!)
```

## Parameters

- **sender**: The web view containing the frame.
- **frame**: The frame being loaded.

## Discussion

This method is invoked when a data source transitions from a provisional to committed state—that is, once the data source of `frame` has received one byte or more of data. This method is invoked after a [doc://com.apple.webkit/documentation/WebKit/WebFrameLoadDelegate/webView(_:didStartProvisionalLoadFor:)] message but before a `webView:didFinishLoadForFrame:` message is sent to the delegate.

In some cases, a single frame load may be committed more than once. This happens in the case of multipart/x-mixed-replace, also known as a “server push.” In this case, a single frame load results in multiple documents loaded in sequence. This method is invoked once for each document that is successfully loaded.
title: webView(_:didCommitLoadFor:)
